name: Grumpy Bookstore Owner
description: A bookstore owner has a store open for n minutes. Every minute, some customers enter. The owner is grumpy[i] = 1 for some minutes (unhappy customers don't count), and grumpy[i] = 0 otherwise. The owner can use a secret technique to not be grumpy for minutes consecutive minutes, but can only use it once. Return the maximum number of satisfied customers.
constraints: n == customers.length == grumpy.length. 1 <= minutes <= n <= 2 * 10^4. 0 <= customers[i] <= 1000. grumpy[i] is 0 or 1.
python_template:
# Grumpy Bookstore Owner

from typing import List

def maxSatisfied(customers: List[int], grumpy: List[int], minutes: int) -> int:
    pass

performance.stress_intent: kill window rescans over 20k-element inputs with long secret-technique intervals
